---
title: "Inundation"
author: "Dana Lanceman"
date: "15/06/2021"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = 'C:/Set/Your/Directory/Here/cumbung_assessment')
```

```{r Set up}
library(sf)
library(raster)
library(exactextractr)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r Load files}
cumbungBoundary <- st_read('raw/shp/Cumbung_ecological.shp')

# put all raster files in the folder into a list
files <- list.files(path = "raw/raster/Inundation_reclass", pattern = "_binary.tif$")
# $ at end means it's the end of the string so won't pick up files with extras at end of extension

files

#import all raster files in folder using lapply
setwd("cumbung_assessment/raw/raster/Inundation_reclass")
allrasters <- lapply(files, raster)
```


```{r Prepare for and calculate area}
# stack rasters
s <- stack(allrasters)

# calculate inundation area - df returns a dataframe
# the st_zm function helped read cumbungBoundary
# need to specify we're using extract from raster and not from another package
ex <- raster::extract(s, st_zm(cumbungBoundary), fun='sum', na.rm=TRUE, df=TRUE)
# remove ID column
ex <- ex[,-1]
# add names to each column based on years of rasters
names(ex) <- gsub("_binary.tif", "", basename(files))
```


```{r Manipulate data}
# Change from wide to long format, remove ID field
ex_long <- pivot_longer(ex, cols = c(1:35), names_to = "year", values_to = "pixels")

# transform these values to areas based on pixel size 25 x 25 m
ex_long$area.m2 <- ex_long$pixels * 25 * 25
ex_long$area.km2 <- ex_long$area.m2 / 1000 / 1000

view(ex_long)

write.csv(ex, file = "output2/annual_inundation_area.csv")
```


```{r With Gilad's data}
# put all Landsat files in the folder into a list
L_files <- list.files(path = "../../Data/LandsatMaxInun", pattern = ".tif$")
# $ at end means it's the end of the string so won't pick up files with extras at end of extension

files

#import all raster files in folder using lapply
setwd("raw/raster/Inundation_reclass")
allrasters <- lapply(files, raster)
```
